accept: Tells agent: run act help on first session to learn mechanics; run act_next to start work; commit with the issue's commit_marker; act close on done.
Lists explicit human-escape conditions (spec ambiguity, breaking-change decisions, scope expansions).
Notes when sub-agents are appropriate (independent issues, disjoint files) and when not (overlapping CLI code).
Keeps recommendations versioned with rationale so a future skill-extraction pass can mine them.
payload.description:
This repo dogfoods act on its own backlog. Without a CLAUDE.md, a fresh agent session here doesn't know to run act_next, doesn't know to use commit markers, doesn't know to file follow-ups via act create instead of TODO comments. CLAUDE.md should be the per-repo rules layer on top of act help — it references act help for mechanics and adds opinions: when to break the loop for human, when to spawn sub-agents, how to handle ambiguity in an issue's acceptance criteria, the test-then-commit-then-close discipline.

Will be revised continuously as we use act on this repo. After 3-5 successful sessions, stable patterns extract into a global skill triggered by .act/ existing.
payload.title: draft CLAUDE.md anchoring the dogfood loop
